---
title: "R & RStudio Setup Guide"
---

## Overview

This page provides a quick-start checklist for setting up your R computing environment. For a comprehensive, step-by-step walkthrough with screenshots and troubleshooting tips, see **Appendix A** of the Lecture Notes:

{{< fa book-open >}} [**Appendix A: R Setup Guide**]({{< var books.lecture_notes >}}/appendices/a-r-setup-guide.html){target="_blank"}


## Quick Setup Checklist

Follow these steps to get up and running. Each step should take only a few minutes.

### 1. Install R

Download and install R from the [Comprehensive R Archive Network (CRAN)](https://cran.r-project.org/):

- **Windows**: Click "Download R for Windows" and run the installer
- **macOS**: Click "Download R for macOS" and open the `.pkg` file
- **Linux**: Follow the instructions for your distribution

::: {.callout-tip}
## Always use the latest version
Make sure you install **R 4.3.0 or later**. Some packages used in this course require a recent version.
:::

### 2. Install RStudio

Download the free Desktop version from [posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/). RStudio provides a user-friendly interface for writing code, viewing plots, and managing files.

### 3. Install Essential Packages

Open RStudio and run the following in the **Console** pane:

```r
# Core packages
install.packages(c("tidyverse", "car", "broom", "marginaleffects"))

# Course datasets package
install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("joonho112/regdatasets")
```

### 4. Verify Your Installation

Run this quick test to confirm everything is working:

```r
library(tidyverse)
library(regdatasets)
data(gcse)
glimpse(gcse)
```

If you see a summary of the `gcse` dataset, you are all set.


## {{< fa cloud >}} Cloud Alternative: Posit Cloud

If you have trouble installing software locally, you can use [Posit Cloud](https://posit.cloud/) (formerly RStudio Cloud) as a browser-based alternative. Posit Cloud runs R and RStudio entirely in your web browser --- no installation required.

::: {.callout-note}
## Free tier limitations
The free Posit Cloud plan provides 25 project hours per month, which should be sufficient for most coursework. If you need more time, a paid plan is available.
:::


## Essential Packages

The table below lists the key R packages used throughout this course.

| Package | Purpose | Used In |
|---------|---------|---------|
| `tidyverse` | Data wrangling and visualization (includes `ggplot2`, `dplyr`, `tidyr`, etc.) | All chapters |
| `regdatasets` | Course-specific datasets ({{< var materials.datasets >}} curated educational datasets) | All chapters |
| `car` | Companion to Applied Regression --- diagnostic tests, Type II/III SS | Chapters 5--9 |
| `broom` | Tidy model output (`tidy()`, `glance()`, `augment()`) | All chapters |
| `ggplot2` | Publication-quality graphics (included in `tidyverse`) | All chapters |
| `marginaleffects` | Marginal effects, predictions, and contrasts | Chapters 4--14 |

::: {.callout-tip}
## Installing all packages at once
You can install all essential packages with a single command:

```r
install.packages(c("tidyverse", "car", "broom", "marginaleffects", "remotes"))
remotes::install_github("joonho112/regdatasets")
```
:::
